data_flow_pipeline.entry_point: User invokes bat CLI -> main() in build/main.rs parses command-line arguments via clap -> creates Config struct with user preferences

stages.responsibility: Parse CLI arguments, create Config, open input sources (files or stdin)
stages.input: Command-line arguments, file paths, stdin stream
stages.output: Input struct(s) containing file/stdin metadata and readers; Config with user preferences

stages.responsibility: Detect file type, select appropriate syntax highlighter and color theme
stages.input: Input metadata (filename, extension, first line), user theme preference
stages.output: SyntaxReference for highlighting rules; Theme for color scheme

stages.responsibility: Read file content, handle encoding (UTF-8, UTF-16), expand tabs, strip ANSI codes, handle non-printable characters
stages.output: Raw text lines, preprocessed text (tabs expanded, ANSI stripped if requested)

stages.responsibility: Apply syntax highlighting rules, compute style attributes, track ANSI escape sequences
stages.input: Preprocessed text lines, SyntaxReference, Theme, StyleComponents
stages.output: Highlighted lines with style metadata, ANSI escape sequences for colors/formatting

stages.responsibility: Add line numbers, grid borders, line change markers, headers, footers; handle wrapping and line ranges
stages.input: Highlighted lines, line range filters, decoration preferences
stages.output: Fully decorated and formatted output with all visual elements

stages.responsibility: Write formatted output to terminal or pager; handle pagination and interactive viewing
stages.input: Decorated output lines, paging mode preference, pager executable
stages.output: Terminal display via stdout or pager process

data_flow_pipeline.exit_point: OutputType::Pager or OutputType::Stdout writes final formatted text to terminal via OutputHandle (stdio or pager process)

input.role: Handles all input sources: files, stdin, byte buffers, custom readers. Manages file opening, encoding detection, line-by-line reading

config.role: Central configuration struct holding all user preferences: paging mode, highlighting ranges, binary behavior, ANSI stripping, wrapping mode, style components

assets.role: Manages syntax definitions and color themes. Loads from binary, cache, or filesystem. Detects syntax from filename, extension, or first-line heuristics

syntax_mapping.role: Maps file extensions and patterns to syntax definitions; handles custom user mappings

theme.role: Resolves theme selection: explicit user choice, system color scheme detection, or defaults; handles dark/light preference

preprocessor.role: Text transformation: expands tabs, replaces non-printable characters with notation, strips ANSI codes, removes overstrike formatting

printer.role: Core rendering engine. SimplePrinter for plain output; InteractivePrinter for highlighted output with decorations

pretty_printer.role: High-level API for printing. Builder pattern for configuring output

controller.role: Orchestrates the full printing pipeline: opens inputs, detects syntax, creates printer, handles errors

decorations.role: Generates visual decorations: line numbers, grid borders, line change markers

style.role: Manages style components: which visual elements to display

vscreen.role: Virtual screen abstraction for ANSI escape sequence handling

output.role: Abstracts output destination: stdout or external pager process

pager.role: Manages pager executable selection and execution

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Opens files and stdin streams specified by user
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: File paths -> Input objects with readers and metadata

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Detects syntax and loads theme based on input metadata
Creates printer instance and delegates line-by-line rendering
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Config, Input, SyntaxReference, Theme -> Formatted output lines

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Preprocesses each line: expands tabs, strips ANSI, replaces non-printable
Parses and applies ANSI escape sequences for syntax highlighting
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Highlighted text with escape codes -> Style attributes and text segments

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Generates line numbers, grid borders, and change markers
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Line number, change status -> Decoration text with styling

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Queries which visual components to display
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: StyleComponents query -> Boolean flags for each component

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Delegates to controller for actual printing after configuration
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Configured Config and Input objects -> Printed output

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Creates output handle for writing results
Initializes pager process if paging is enabled
Queries custom syntax mappings for file extensions
Resolves theme preference and detects system color scheme
Indirectly: InputReader provides raw lines that printer preprocesses
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Raw file bytes -> Text lines (via printer)

extension_proposal.feature: Custom output formatter plugin system (e.g., JSON export, HTML, Markdown)
required_changes: Create new module: output_formatters/ with trait OutputFormatter
Modify printer.rs: extract rendering logic into formatter-agnostic intermediate representation
Modify pretty_printer.rs: expose formatter selection in builder API
integration_points: After syntax highlighting completes: pass styled line segments to formatter
In printer.rs print_line(): emit formatter-agnostic events
In output.rs: route formatted events to appropriate OutputType handler
